title: When exporting a gantt view to pdf use the title of the view in the pdf instead of the word gantt
excerpt: When i export a ‘gantt view’ from a project board to pdf it uses the title ‘gantt’ on the pdf not the title of the view (eg; Current Projects).  I want to use the exported pdf as part of a report but I then have to edit …

posts.author: HaideeB (Haidee Barrow)
posts.cookedHtml: When i export a ‘gantt view’ from a project board to pdf it uses the title ‘gantt’ on the pdf not the title of the view (eg; Current Projects). I want to use the exported pdf as part of a report but I then have to edit the pdf and rename the title from gantt to the appropriate title.

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this is possible with The PDF Maker (app.thepdfmaker.com).

With The PDF Maker, you’re not restricted by default labels or view titles like “Gantt.” You can fully customize your PDF template—including titles, headings, and layout—using mapped data from your Monday.com board. So, instead of a generic “Gantt” title, you can dynamically insert your own view title (like “Current Projects”) or any other label that reflects the context of the export.

Every new response triggers PDF generation automatically.

Please view the support article below:

help.thepdfmaker.com

Automate Documents with the Monday App - Full Guide | The PDF Maker Help Center

Master document automation using the Monday app and PDF Maker. Streamline workflows and enhance productivity by effortlessly generating PDFs directly from Monday.com.
